tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent ~$118.20 post-deadline; WTI ~$120.50; limited US-Israel strikes have begun but Kharg remains untouched, preserving further upside. Tehran Toll Booth stays active; AIS transits remain down about 95%; Qatari LNG restrictions persist. Tail risk rises to 60%. Stress range shifts to $110-145/bbl, with $160-185/bbl still credible if phase-two strikes hit export infrastructure. PTTEP and Medco remain the cleanest ASEAN equity beta while ASEAN importers absorb the new shock leg.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent ~$118.20 and iTraxx Asia IG ~206bp place the post-deadline move into a more systemic phase. ASEAN Energy Equities +3.4% while ASEAN Banks -2.7% as spread widening and weaker FX start to hit funding assumptions. Limited strikes keep the banking underweight intact because the event now extends beyond deadline-risk positioning into sustained balance-sheet stress. Trim the bank underweight only on confirmed commercial Hormuz reopening and iTraxx back below 170bp.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short - Dip Opportunity on Trump-Driven Rally
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP ~59.35 as the bilateral safe-passage deal fails to insulate Manila from a renewed oil and LNG spike. Brent ~$118.20 and JKM ~$23.60 keep the Philippine import bill under acute pressure despite preferential routing. Post-deadline strikes confirm the bilateral framework is only a freight cushion, not a macro shield. Stress target 61.00-62.00 remains intact; hold until there is confirmed de-escalation plus a visible recovery in Hormuz transit counts.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short - Re-Entry on Trump Pause Dip
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR ~17,120 with Indonesia still outside Iran's friendly-nations routing framework. Brent ~$118.20 is now roughly $48/bbl above Indonesia's ICP budget assumption while Indonesia 10Y widens to 7.42%. Post-deadline strikes shift the move from event premium to fiscal stress. Stress target 17,200-17,800 remains live, with 18,000+ still the tail path if export infrastructure is hit next. Stop only on a confirmed ceasefire plus a material oil retracement.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection - Hold After Deadline
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia IG ~206bp and ASEAN HY ~592bp confirm the deadline has converted into active credit repricing rather than a one-night binary event. Tehran Toll Booth economics remain incompatible with normal P&I restoration, and limited strikes leave ample room for a second, larger wave. 215-230bp remains the next target band if Kharg or broader export infrastructure is hit. Keep CDS protection on; the hedge window has not closed.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF - Hold Into Phase Two
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM ~$23.60/MMBtu and TTF ~EUR56.80/MWh keep Asia tightest on relative scarcity. Qatari LNG restrictions remain in place after the deadline, and limited strikes on Iranian infrastructure do nothing to restore Ras Laffan confidence or normal Hormuz passage. JKM upside target stays $24-28/MMBtu in the near term, with further upside if export infrastructure is targeted. Stop only on a confirmed Ras Laffan restart and commercial Hormuz reopening.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's refinancing window is now effectively shut. iTraxx Asia IG ~206bp and ASEAN HY ~592bp reflect post-deadline credit repricing after limited strikes began. Tail risk is 60%. Move immediately to secured bank lines, private credit, and DFI-backed structures; the pre-strike window is gone.

PTTEP: THB window remains closed - TH 10Y ~2.38% and USD/THB ~34.10 after post-deadline strikes begin. Brent ~$118.20 boosts upstream EBITDA materially, but the funding market is deteriorating. Execute H2 2026 prefunding via USD now; Gulf JV toll-booth and sanctions-compliance review remains urgent.

Expand USD revolving-credit capacity immediately. Brent ~$118.20 and JKM ~$23.60 are now structurally higher, USD/SGD is ~1.2955, and iTraxx Asia IG has widened near 206bp. The post-deadline escalation the brief warned about is now live. Execute revolving-credit expansion and USD hedging immediately; waiting for calmer markets is no longer a valid assumption.

Hyflux must activate restructuring immediately. Base 5%, stress 35%, tail 60%; Brent ~$118.20; ASEAN HY ~592bp; JKM ~$23.60. Kuwait desalination remains the clearest regional precedent for utility stress under active conflict. The deadline has passed and the crisis has escalated into sustained disruption. Pursue private-credit and quasi-sovereign structures now.
